title: Senior Accountant (Group Reporting & Consolidation)
description:
• SGX Listed - Well Establish Company

  • Basic $4000 to $5000 + VB

  • Excellent Welfare and Benefits + Career progression

  • Working location: Tuas Crescent (Company Transport Provided)

  • Working day: Monday – Friday

  • Working hours: 8.30am – 5.15pm

Key Responsibilities

  • Coordinate and liaise with subsidiaries on all group consolidation matters.

  • Ensure timely and accurate submission of consolidation packages by subsidiaries.

  • Review subsidiary financial submissions to ensure completeness, accuracy, and compliance with Group requirements.

  • Prepare monthly, quarterly, and annual consolidated financial statements in accordance with IFRS and Group reporting standards.

  • Prepare consolidation supporting schedules, group cash flow statements, and management reporting schedules.

  • Assist in streamlining and improving consolidation processes and reporting templates.

  • Support the preparation of SGX announcements, including quarter-end, half-year, and year-end financial disclosures (numerical information).

  • Assist in the consolidation of the Group’s annual budget and forecasts.

  • Coordinate and manage external audit matters, including responding to audit requests and liaising with auditors.

  • Ensure compliance with IFRS/SFRS, statutory requirements, and relevant regulatory guidelines.

  • Support the development and implementation of standard operating procedures (SOPs).

  • Coordinate the preparation of Board papers and support quarterly Board reporting.

  • Assist in the preparation of annual financial statements, including notes to accounts.

  • Support the preparation and publication of the annual report, AGM, and EGM documentation, including coordination with auditors, designers, and typesetters.

  • Review disclosures for accuracy, consistency, and regulatory compliance.

  • Support the preparation of the Group’s Sustainability Report in line with SGX requirements and applicable ESG frameworks.

  • Coordinate with subsidiaries to collect, consolidate, and validate ESG and climate-related data.

  • Monitor and track sustainability initiatives and performance indicators.

  • Perform ad-hoc duties as assigned.

Job Requirements

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field/Professional accounting certifications (ACCA/CPA/CA SG)

  • Min 3 years of relevant experience in accounting, audit, or finance.

  • Experience in Financial reporting (IFRS/SFRS), Consolidation, management report packages, Internal controls/governance, working with external auditors, drafting financial statements & preparing annual report, AGM and EGM, preparing budgets and forecasts.



Candidates are encouraged to apply this position via Apply Now button with the following information in the resume



• Work experiences and job responsibilities

• Current and Expected salary

• Reason for leaving

• Date of availability

• Education background



We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.



NG JYA YEE (R23119014)

EA Recruitment Pte Ltd

EA License No: 21C0492
